title: aristolochia hockii subsp. tuberculata
intro: Aristolochia hockii subsp. tuberculata, commonly known as the tubercled Dutchman's pipe, is a species of flowering plant in the Aristolochiaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Aristolochia hockii subsp. tuberculata include the potential treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, although its effectiveness and safety for human consumption have not been extensively studied. Aristolochia hockii subsp. tuberculata is often prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, with the dried roots and rhizomes typically being used for medicinal purposes.
